params.text:
What active listening is

Listening is more than staying quiet while someone speaks. Active listening means giving the person your full attention and showing that you are taking in what they say. It tells the person that they matter and helps you understand what they really mean, not just the words on the surface.

To listen actively, face the person, make comfortable eye contact and put distractions aside. Do not interrupt or finish their sentences. Let there be pauses; silence gives people time to think, especially if they find talking difficult or tiring.

Active listening takes real effort and concentration. It is easy to drift off, plan your reply or worry about your next task, but the person will sense it if your attention wanders.

Showing you have understood

Good listeners check that they have understood correctly. Small responses keep the conversation going and prove you are engaged without taking over.

  • Reflect: repeat back key points in your own words.
  • Clarify: ask gentle questions if something is unclear.
  • Encourage: nod, or say a quiet yes, to show you are following.
  • Notice feelings: listen for the emotion behind the words.

Open questions that begin with what, how or tell me draw out more than questions with a simple yes or no answer. Resist the urge to jump in with advice; sometimes a person just needs to be heard.

params.question: Which of these is the best example of active listening?

answers.text: Facing the person, letting them finish, then reflecting back what you heard to check you understood.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Correct. Full attention plus reflecting back shows respect and confirms understanding.

answers.text: Tidying the room while they talk so you do not waste any time.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Doing other tasks signals the person is not your priority and means you will miss cues.

answers.text: Finishing their sentences to move the conversation along.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Finishing sentences can put words in their mouth and stops them expressing themselves.

answers.text: Nodding along while quietly planning your reply.

tipsAndFeedback.chosenFeedback: Planning your response means you are not truly taking in what is being said.
